title: UNC6426/QUIETVAULT: Compromised nx npm Package Weaponizes Locally-Installed LLM Coding Tools for Credential Theft and AWS Admin Takeover
coverage_detail: vectimus-supchain-010 blocks agent-initiated npm install commands for packages not verified against the hash allowlist, preventing installation of compromised nx package versions that deliver the QUIETVAULT postinstall credential stealer. When an AI coding assistant executes npm install as a package_operation or shell_command, Vectimus intercepts the call before execution and verifies the package hash. vectimus-secrets-001 blocks subsequent file_read calls targeting .env files; vectimus-secrets-002 blocks reads of .ssh/*, .aws/credentials, and .npmrc; vectimus-secrets-003 blocks reads of files in secrets directories and token files — providing defence-in-depth against QUIETVAULT's LLM-assisted credential harvesting phase. Out-of-scope components: postinstall scripts triggered by human-initiated npm install (outside the agent tool-call path) and QUIETVAULT's direct environment-variable enumeration via Node.js process.env do not produce Cedar-visible tool calls and cannot be intercepted by pretool hooks.

sources.title: UNC6426 Exploits nx npm Supply-Chain Attack to Gain AWS Admin Access in 72 Hours — The Hacker News
NPM Supply Chain Attacks: Hidden Risks for AI Agents — Oligo Security

summary: Newly disclosed in March 2026, UNC6426 is a sophisticated threat actor who exploited a compromised nx npm package (August 2025 initial compromise via a Pwn Request technique on the nx repository's pull_request_target workflow) to deliver the QUIETVAULT JavaScript credential stealer via a postinstall hook. QUIETVAULT's distinguishing feature is that it weaponizes any LLM coding tool installed on the endpoint — including Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, and Gemini CLI — by submitting natural-language prompts that direct the AI assistant to scan the system for environment variables, authentication tokens, and cloud credentials, exfiltrating findings to attacker-controlled GitHub repositories. Post-exfiltration, UNC6426 used the stolen GitHub PAT to run the Nord Stream utility, extracted temporary AWS STS tokens for the 'Actions-CloudFormation' IAM role, deployed a CloudFormation stack creating a new role with AdministratorAccess, and achieved full AWS administrator privileges within 72 hours. The attacker subsequently enumerated and accessed S3 buckets, terminated production EC2 and RDS instances, decrypted application keys, and renamed all internal GitHub repositories to '/s1ngularity-repository-[random]' and made them public. vectimus-supchain-010 was written in response to this incident class and blocks agent-initiated npm installs of unverified packages; vectimus-secrets policies block the AI-assisted credential harvesting phase.

title: Claude Fraud Malware Campaign: Trojanized VS Code Extension and Malvertising Deliver MacSync Infostealer Targeting Developers (15,600 Victims)
coverage_detail: The Claude Fraud campaign operates entirely outside Vectimus's tool-call enforcement boundary. Attack vector 1 (malvertising ClickFix): Google-sponsored ads serve fake Claude documentation pages that instruct developers to paste encoded terminal commands to install MacSync. This social engineering attack occurs in the developer's browser before any agent is running; no Vectimus pretool hook is registered at this point. Attack vector 2 (trojanized VS Code extension): a malicious extension masquerading as the official Claude Code plugin executes PowerShell silently to modify Windows Defender exclusions and download payloads. This is a software installation attack targeting the developer's machine directly via VS Code's Node.js runtime, not an agent tool call. No existing Vectimus Cedar policy can intercept software installation via fake documentation clicks, malicious VS Code extensions, or browser-based social engineering. This is architecturally out of scope for any tool-call governance product.
gap_description: Both attack vectors — malvertising-delivered ClickFix terminal commands and trojanized VS Code extensions — execute before or outside the Vectimus agent tool-call pipeline. These are pre-initialization attacks on the developer's host environment. No Cedar policy can block downloads from fake documentation sites or execution of malicious VS Code extension code.

sources.title: Claude Fraud: When Trusted Tools Become the Attack Surface — 7AI Blog
Critical Vulnerabilities Found in GitHub Copilot, Gemini CLI, Claude, and Other AI Tools Affect Millions — GBHackers

summary: The Claude Fraud malware campaign, active through at least March 17, 2026, targets AI developers via two confirmed attack vectors. Vector 1 (macOS): Google-sponsored search ads lead to fake Claude documentation pages hosted on attacker-controlled domains (including Squarespace-hosted variants), instructing users to paste encoded terminal commands (ClickFix technique) that deliver the MacSync infostealer — which targets macOS Keychain credentials, browser data, and cryptocurrency wallet keys, exfiltrating via HTTP POST to hardcoded C2 infrastructure. Vector 2 (Windows): a trojanized VS Code extension masquerading as the official Claude Code plugin executes PowerShell silently via VS Code's Node.js runtime, modifying Windows Defender exclusions and downloading payloads via LOLBIN (mshta.exe). Three confirmed enterprise incidents were identified (one macOS, two Windows at a large tech company). Combined with public research from Moonlock Lab and AdGuard, over 15,600 victims are documented. C2 domains include a2abotnet[.]com and the wildcard *.official-version[.]com. The campaign is entirely outside Vectimus's enforcement boundary — both attack vectors fire before any agent tool-call pipeline is active.

title: TeamPCP/CanisterWorm Supply Chain Attack: Backdoored Trivy VS Code Extension Injects Hidden Prompts into AI Coding Assistants to Exfiltrate Credentials via GitHub CLI
coverage_detail: vectimus-supchain-010 blocks agent-initiated npm install of packages not on the hash-verified allowlist, providing defence-in-depth against CanisterWorm propagation when an AI coding assistant attempts to run npm install within affected projects. vectimus-secrets-001, vectimus-secrets-002, and vectimus-secrets-003 block AI coding assistant file_read calls targeting credential paths (.env, .ssh/*, .aws/credentials, .npmrc, token files), mitigating the credential harvesting phase if QUIETVAULT-style prompts direct the assistant to read credential files directly. Gap 1: the primary exfiltration vector — GitHub CLI shell commands (gh auth token, gh api, gh issue create) directed by injected natural-language prompts — is not explicitly covered by existing shell_command policies; these commands produce shell_command tool calls that bypass current credential-read patterns. Gap 2: the root injection vector (hidden prompt instructions embedded in the backdoored Trivy VS Code extension versions 1.8.12–1.8.13) is an LLM input manipulation issue that occurs before any Cedar tool-call hook is evaluated and is outside Cedar's enforcement boundary. Gap 3: postinstall-triggered systemd backdoor persistence and ICP canister-based C2 polling operate at the OS layer outside the agent tool-call pipeline.
gap_description: GitHub CLI shell commands used for credential exfiltration (gh auth token, gh api to create issues with stolen data payloads) are not matched by existing shell_command policies. The hidden prompt injection delivery mechanism — malicious natural-language instructions embedded in a backdoored VS Code extension — is an LLM input manipulation attack outside Cedar's enforcement boundary. OS-level persistence (systemd backdoor, ICP canister C2 polling) is also outside scope.

sources.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: IOCs, Affected Versions, and Remediation — Geordie AI Security Advisory
When Security Scanners Become the Weapon: Breaking Down the Trivy Supply Chain Attack — Palo Alto Networks
Trivy Supply Chain Attack Triggers Self-Spreading CanisterWorm Across 47 npm Packages — The Hacker News

summary: Between March 19 and March 24, 2026, threat actor TeamPCP (also tracked as DeadCatx3/PCPcat/ShellForce/CipherForce) executed a five-phase supply chain attack beginning with credential reuse against Aqua Security's aqua-bot service account. Phase 3 targeted AI coding assistants directly: the threat actor backdoored the Trivy VS Code extension (versions 1.8.12–1.8.13) with hidden natural-language prompts directing locally installed AI coding assistants — Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Gemini CLI, OpenAI Codex, and Kiro — to collect system information and authentication tokens and exfiltrate them via the victim's GitHub CLI. Phase 5 (CanisterWorm) deployed a self-propagating npm worm that expanded to 66+ packages (141 malicious artifacts), used a novel ICP blockchain canister as a dead-drop C2 resolver, and established persistent systemd backdoors. The campaign subsequently expanded to Checkmarx GitHub Actions workflows (CVE-2026-33634, CVSS 9.4) and LiteLLM PyPI packages (v1.82.7–1.82.8). Safe versions: Trivy v0.69.3, trivy-action v0.35.0. Any developer whose CI/CD pipeline ran affected Trivy versions or who installed affected npm packages should rotate all accessible credentials immediately.
recommended_policy_description: In the exfil pack, add a shell_command rule blocking GitHub CLI commands commonly used as AI-agent-directed exfiltration channels. Conditions should cover: context.command like '*gh auth token*' (retrieves stored GitHub authentication token); context.command like '*gh api*' when combined with POST-method flags (context.command like '*--method POST*' or context.command like '*-X POST*'); and context.command like '*gh issue create*' or context.command like '*gh gist create*' when the command body references environment-variable patterns or credential-file content. This closes the gap where hidden prompt injection directs AI coding assistants to exfiltrate stolen credentials via the GitHub CLI — a vector not covered by existing file_read-focused secret access policies or the web_request-focused EXAG-001 rule.

title: CVE-2026-33017: Langflow Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution in Public Flow Build Endpoint Exploited Within 20 Hours of Disclosure
coverage_detail: Langflow is not a supported Vectimus agent platform; Vectimus integrates with Claude Code, Cursor, and GitHub Copilot via native hook integrations. CVE-2026-33017 is an unauthenticated HTTP-level attack targeting Langflow's POST /api/v1/build_public_tmp/{flow_id}/flow endpoint directly — the attacker submits a crafted JSON payload containing arbitrary Python code in node definitions, which Langflow executes server-side without sandboxing. This attack does not pass through any Cedar pretool hook and is entirely outside Vectimus's enforcement boundary. No existing Cedar policy governs Langflow's public-facing API endpoints or its Python execution layer. There is no Cedar policy for this class of agentic framework server-side RCE.
gap_description: Langflow is outside the Vectimus-supported agent set. The attack vector (unauthenticated HTTP POST to a public API endpoint) bypasses any tool-call pipeline. Vectimus cannot enforce policy on Langflow's server-side Python execution. This is architecturally out of scope.

sources.title: CVE-2026-33017: How Attackers Compromised Langflow AI Pipelines in 20 Hours — Sysdig
AI Coding Agents Keep Repeating Decade-Old Security Mistakes — Help Net Security

summary: CVE-2026-33017 is a critical unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in Langflow, the open-source visual framework for building AI agents and RAG pipelines. The flaw exists in the POST /api/v1/build_public_tmp/{flow_id}/flow endpoint, which accepts attacker-supplied flow data containing arbitrary Python code in node definitions and executes it server-side without sandboxing — requiring no authentication. The advisory was published on March 17, 2026 at 20:05 UTC; the Sysdig Threat Research Team observed the first exploitation attempt within 20 hours (March 18 at 16:04 UTC), built from the advisory description alone before any public PoC existed. In the 48 hours following disclosure, six unique attacker IPs were observed: automated nuclei scanner probes executing id commands, custom bash shell delivery from staged attacker infrastructure, and credential harvesting targeting environment variables containing database connection strings, API keys, and cloud credentials (including Anthropic API keys and AWS credentials). Compromised Langflow instances provide lateral access to all connected cloud accounts and data stores. Langflow operators should apply the patched version immediately and audit environment variables for any exposure.
